components.name: Beam (Wide Flange)
components.description: Fireproofed beam interrupted by penetrating element (hanger rod, pipe, conduit, or similar)

properties.sfrm_status: Continuous SFRM except at penetration zone

components.name: Hanger Rod / Pipe Penetration
components.material: Steel rod, steel/copper pipe, or EMT conduit
components.description: Element penetrating through or attached to the fireproofed beam. Creates a discontinuity in the SFRM envelope.

properties.attachment_method: Beam clamp, welded tab, drilled hole, or powder-actuated fastener

components.name: SFRM Interrupted Zone
components.description: Area where SFRM is missing or reduced due to the penetrating element. This zone must be repaired/supplemented.

damage_cause: SFRM applied around penetration with voids

components.name: Supplemental Protection (Intumescent Collar or Wrap)
components.material: Intumescent firestop collar, intumescent wrap strip, or firestop sealant
components.description: Supplemental fire protection at the penetration to restore fire rating. Method depends on penetration type and UL/FM listing.

properties.collar_type: Intumescent collar (for pipe penetrations through rated assembly)
properties.wrap_type: Intumescent wrap strip (for hanger rods and small penetrations)
properties.listing_required: Must be installed per tested and listed firestop system (UL/FM)

assembly_constraints.description: Fire rating must be restored at every penetration through or attachment to fireproofed members
assembly_constraints.rule: Each penetration requires a listed firestop system or engineering judgment (EJ) to restore the fire rating

assembly_constraints.description: SFRM must be repaired/reapplied as close to penetration as possible
assembly_constraints.rule: Patch SFRM tight to penetrating element, then apply supplemental firestop per listing

assembly_constraints.description: Ideal sequence: SFRM first, then penetrations with firestop. Reality: penetrations often occur before SFRM.
assembly_constraints.rule: If penetrations installed before SFRM, spray around them. If installed after, repair SFRM and add firestop.

assembly_constraints.description: Each penetration firestop must be documented with the UL/FM system number used
assembly_constraints.rule: Maintain firestop log per IBC Section 714.3.1.2 — photo documentation recommended

assembly_constraints.description: Hanger attachment must not compromise fire rating of supporting member
assembly_constraints.rule: Beam clamps preferred over welded attachments. If welded, SFRM must cover weld and attachment hardware.
